title: Constant-time key comparison in webhook handler
description:
LOCATION: src/webhook.rs:55-59

FINDING: The webhook key comparison hashes both the expected and provided keys with SHA-256 before calling ct_eq, rather than directly comparing the key strings. This correctly prevents timing side-channels that could leak the key length or byte values through branch timing. Hashing before comparison also means the comparison time is constant (32 bytes) regardless of input length, preventing a length-oracle attack via the ct_eq result.

This is precisely the right pattern for secret-bearing HTTP authentication tokens and should be preserved if someone proposes 'simplifying' to a direct == comparison or even a naive hmac check.
close_reason: Acknowledged: webhook ct_eq design is correct
